cluster_label: Threading, snipping, drawing and dressing hands
educational_objective: Hands want smaller neater challenges — thread, lace, copy shapes. Large supervised pieces.
age_stage_nuance: Versus earlier bands: neater two-hand control and pattern copying, not grab-and-post. For 34–36m FF gates, prefer 18m+/2+/24m+ chunky sets — classic 3+ / not-suitable-under-3 crates fail because band min is 34 months.
what_to_look_for: Chunky beads (about 4 cm+) or large fruit/animal pieces; tipped or short safety-release cords; Ages 2+ / 18m+ / 24m+ marks; pattern cards where possible; supervised table play.
what_to_avoid: Tiny jewellery kits; fuse beads; products marked only 3+ or not suitable under 3 years; long unsupervised cords; unverified marketplace-only brands.
methodology: Re-researched after FF quarantine fails (dead URLs, missing ratings, unparsed ages, and 3+ marks). Prioritised live brand/specialist primary URLs that smoke 200, Ages 2+/18m+/12m+ marks that overlap 34–36m, rating ≥4.4 with ≥15 reviews where available, and specialist exemptions with written reasons for nursery/Djeco/Moulin Roty stockists. Explicitly rejected Melissa and Doug, Bigjigs, Jaques, Galt 3+ crates for age-gate conflict.

key_specs.included_items: 26 dual-sided letter beads, 2 laces, storage jar, scoop

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Twenty-six chunky 4.5 cm letter sweets with upper and lowercase sides, two laces, a scoop and a storage jar for supervised threading.
top_picks.ember_verdict: At nearly three, hands want a neater job than posting. These oversized letter sweets are properly chunky — not jewellery beads — so threading stays a table game you can sit beside. Brand mark is Ages 2+, which fits this band; keep sessions supervised and shorten cords when you pack away.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be spotting familiar letters while finishing a short laced row to show you
top_picks.why_it_fits: Large bead size clears the 34–36m age-safety preference; letter faces add a light copy-and-name layer without tiny craft pieces.
top_picks.caveats: Amazon UK also prints a generic under-36 choking line on some marketplaces — we follow the Learning Resources / Primary ICT Ages 2–6 mark and oversized 4.5 cm pieces. Laces are short; supervise always.
top_picks.gift_note: Strong gift if the child already notices letters; jar presentation wraps cleanly.
top_picks.ownership_note: Skip if you already own oversized letter lacing sweets; do not double up with tiny alphabet jewellery kits.
top_picks.safety_notes: Supervised play only. Keep laces short during use and store out of reach when finished. Chunky sweets reduce swallow risk versus craft beads, but cords still need an adult beside them.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Solid UK review base; parents praise size and letter play; occasional notes on short laces or damaged outer boxes.
evidence_sources: Learning Resources brand spec via educational stockists
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Plastic holds up; check lace tips and full letter set.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Primary URL smoke 200 on 2026-07-22. Age taken from Primary ICT Ages 2-6 (not Amazon under-36 boilerplate).
top_picks.consensus_corroboration: Learning Resources nursery staple; repeated across UK educational suppliers.
top_picks.editorial_mentions: Parent blog reviews of Smart Snacks ABC Lacing Sweets as fine-motor + letter play.

key_specs.included_items: Two caterpillar strings with leaf stoppers, 13 wooden fruit pieces

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Two wooden caterpillar cords and thirteen chunky fruit pieces to thread, with leaf stoppers so fruit stays on the string.
top_picks.ember_verdict: This is threading with a job — feed the caterpillar — which suits a nearly-three who wants a finished line to show you. Pieces are large wooden fruits, not craft beads, and the brand mark is 18 months+. Sit together; cords still need supervision.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be naming fruit aloud and wanting both caterpillars laced end to end
top_picks.why_it_fits: Narrative threading matches the busy-hands cluster without jumping to 3+ jewellery-size beads.
top_picks.caveats: Amazon UK listing can show out of stock; buy via UK wooden-toy stockists if needed. Pair with The Very Hungry Caterpillar book only if you already own it — not required.
top_picks.gift_note: Cute gift angle without feeling like a jewellery kit.
top_picks.ownership_note: Different format from open bead crates — complements rather than duplicates.
top_picks.safety_notes: Supervised play only. Brand age 18 months+. Cord length is the main hazard — stay beside them and put cords away after play.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Very strong rating; themes praise wood quality, fruit size and fine-motor focus. UK-local reviews thinner because stock is patchy.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Wooden fruit holds up; check paint and leaf stoppers.
top_picks.substitute_if_unavailable: Moulin Roty Les Toupitis lacing beads
top_picks.evidence_notes: Brand URL smoke 200 on 2026-07-22. Age from Hape 18M+ mark. Ratings from Amazon UK listing.
top_picks.consensus_corroboration: Surfaces across Hape catalogues and international Amazon ratings for the same E1072 SKU.
top_picks.editorial_mentions: Often paired with Hungry Caterpillar book play in parent reviews.

key_specs.included_items: 24 chunky beads (stars, circles, squares), 2 fabric laces

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Twenty-four chunky wooden stars, circles and squares with two fabric laces in a small tub — a first threading set from 18 months.
top_picks.ember_verdict: If you want proper wooden beads without the 3+ crate warning, Filacolor is the calm UK specialist option. Shapes are large enough for nearly-three fingers and the mark is 18 months+. Still sit together — fabric laces tangle fast.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be sorting stars from circles before threading a short colour run
top_picks.why_it_fits: True 18m+ chunky wooden threading that clears the band age gate where Melissa and Doug / Bigjigs 3+ sets fail.
top_picks.caveats: Fewer mass-market Amazon ratings than Melissa and Doug; buy from a named UK Djeco stockist. Not a pattern-card set.
top_picks.gift_note: Compact gift that still feels intentional.
top_picks.ownership_note: Enough if you only need one small bead tub; skip if you already own oversized wooden stars/circles.
top_picks.safety_notes: Supervised play only. 18 months+ mark. Keep fabric laces short in use and store away from younger siblings.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Limited aggregated retail ratings; strong brand + clear chunky-bead specs on UK specialist pages.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Check bead count and lace condition.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Specialist exemption: Djeco early-learning SKU with explicit 18 months+ on live UK specialist pages (smoke 200). Chunky stars/circles/squares sized for toddler hands; no jewellery-kit piece count. Written reason for missing mass-market rating aggregate.
top_picks.consensus_corroboration: Repeated across independent UK Djeco stockists for the same DJ06160 code.
top_picks.editorial_mentions: Appears in early-learning wooden threading roundups via specialist toy shops.

key_specs.materials: Beech wood, MDF cards, fabric laces
key_specs.included_items: 21 colourful wooden beads, 12 pattern/sequence cards, 2 laces

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Twenty-one chunky wooden beads with twelve sequence cards and two laces so children can free-thread or copy a pictured pattern.
top_picks.ember_verdict: This is the pattern-copying pick the Stage 2 card asks for — without a 3+ bead crate. Cards turn lacing into “make it match”, which is the shift at nearly three. Adult beside them; pack cards and laces away together.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be matching a card colour run then wanting you to check it is the same
top_picks.why_it_fits: Only 18m+ set in this shortlist that includes sequence cards for copy-shapes play.
top_picks.caveats: Premium price; fewer Amazon-style ratings. Confirm live UK stock before promising as a gift.
top_picks.gift_note: Gift-forward Moulin Roty presentation with a clear play job.
top_picks.ownership_note: Skip if you already own a pattern-card bead set; otherwise this replaces a plain bead tub.
top_picks.safety_notes: Supervised play only. 18 months+ mark. Sequence cards are not for chewing; keep laces short and store after play.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Specialist stockist evidence; limited public star aggregates on UK Amazon.
evidence_sources: The Blue Zebra UK product page
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Check card set completeness and bead count.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Specialist exemption: Moulin Roty Les Toupitis with explicit 18+ months on live UK retailer page (smoke 200) plus pattern cards that map to the copy-shapes educational objective. Chunky wooden beads, not jewellery kits.
top_picks.consensus_corroboration: Consistent 18m+ and card+bead contents across Moulin Roty stockists.
top_picks.editorial_mentions: Featured in independent wooden-toy shop early-learning ranges.

key_specs.dimensions: Beads about 4.5 cm diameter; laces about 15 cm

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Ten chunky 4.5 cm textured sensory beads with four short laces that use quick-release safety connectors for supervised threading.
top_picks.ember_verdict: If cord worry is the blocker, this is the set with short safety-release laces and properly large beads. It is marked from 12 months, so it overlaps 34–36m cleanly. Still supervised — texture invites mouthing.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be rolling textured beads first, then threading two or three onto a short lace
top_picks.why_it_fits: Large beads + safety-release short cords are the safety-forward answer when classic 3+ wooden crates fail the band gate.
top_picks.caveats: Less pattern-copy depth than Moulin Roty cards; fewer beads than a wooden crate. Nursery-channel pricing.
top_picks.gift_note: Practical gift for parents who are nervous about long cords.
top_picks.ownership_note: Complements a wooden bead set rather than replacing pattern play.
top_picks.safety_notes: Supervised play only. Short safety-release laces reduce strangulation risk versus long craft cords — still stay beside them and store after play. 12 months+ mark on Halilit / TTS.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Education-channel product with clear specs; no large Amazon star aggregate found.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Check lace clips still release cleanly.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Specialist exemption: UK education suppliers (TTS + Halilit) list oversized 4.5 cm beads with safety-release laces and 12 months+ age — nursery staple proof where mass-market star counts are thin. Primary URL smoke 200 on 2026-07-22.
top_picks.consensus_corroboration: Stocked by multiple UK early-years suppliers under Edushape / Halilit.
top_picks.editorial_mentions: Common in EYFS fine-motor resource lists via education catalogues.

guidance_notes.title: Buy / borrow / hold off
guidance_notes.body: Buy one oversized 18m+/2+ threading set first (ABC sweets or Filacolor). Borrow a pattern-card set if grandparents already have one. Hold off on classic Melissa and Doug / Bigjigs 3+ crates for this band unless the child is firmly past 36 months and you accept the retailer under-3 warning.

guidance_notes.title: Supervision
guidance_notes.body: Every Top Pick needs supervised play notes: shorten cords, sit beside threading, store laces away from younger siblings.

qa_summary.notes: Picks 1–2 meet hard rating thresholds. Picks 3–5 use evidence_exemption specialist with written reasons (Djeco / Moulin Roty / Edushape education-channel). All Top Picks avoid 3+ / under-3 exclusions. Primary URLs smoke-checked 2026-07-22.

required_fix_before_ingestion: Run stage3-ff-check.mjs — confirm age_signals pass and specialist exemptions accepted
Confirm Hape UK stock before promising pick 2 as gift
ingestion_ready.founder_review_notes: Re-research focused on clearing FF age gate for 34–36m. Classic 3+ wooden crates moved to skips.
